data.title: Automate verified Windows distribution
Add Windows to the CI test matrix
data.body:
## Goal
- Run the repository's full Go test suite on a native Windows runner for every pull request and push to `main`.
- Make Windows regressions block merges just as macOS and Linux regressions do.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` includes `windows-latest` in the test matrix alongside Ubuntu and macOS.
- The Windows job runs `go mod tidy`, verifies that the tree stays clean, and runs `go test -race -v ./...` unless a documented Go or runner limitation requires the narrowest platform-specific adjustment.
- The Windows job exercises the portable file-locking, storage, command, and integration tests added for v1.2.0.
- Windows is not marked `continue-on-error`, and the existing Linux, macOS, lint, and build jobs keep their current coverage.

## Validation Gates
- `task ci`
- Push the implementation branch and confirm `gh pr checks <pr>` reports passing Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows test jobs.

data.title: Configure automated WinGet manifest pull requests
data.body:
## Goal
- Extend the existing GoReleaser release path so each version tag generates a WinGet manifest and opens an upstream manifest pull request.
- Keep the version, archive URLs, architectures, and checksums derived from the same release artifacts already produced by GoReleaser.

## Context
- Stable package identifier: `Sandover.Ergo`.
- Publisher: `sandover`.
- Manifest fork: `sandover/winget-pkgs`; upstream: `microsoft/winget-pkgs`, branch `master`.
- Use GoReleaser's native `winget` publisher instead of a parallel manifest-generation script.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `.goreleaser.yaml` defines a `winget` recipe for `Sandover.Ergo`, MIT license, project/support URLs, and the existing Windows AMD64 and ARM64 ZIP archives.
- The recipe pushes a per-version branch to `sandover/winget-pkgs` and opens a pull request against `microsoft/winget-pkgs:master`.
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` passes a dedicated `WINGET_TOKEN` secret to GoReleaser without exposing it in repository files or logs.
- The manifest version, URLs, and SHA-256 hashes come from GoReleaser artifacts; no second version or checksum source is introduced.
- Existing GitHub Release and Homebrew outputs remain unchanged.
- The pre-existing `brews` deprecation is left out of scope unless it blocks the WinGet configuration; any required migration must preserve the current Homebrew install path.

## Validation Gates
- `goreleaser check` reports no new configuration errors beyond the known `brews` deprecation.
- `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` succeeds and still produces all six macOS, Linux, and Windows archives.
- Inspect GoReleaser's generated WinGet data or a non-publishing test run and confirm package ID, architectures, archive URLs, and hashes before enabling the secret.
- `task ci`

data.title: Bootstrap the Sandover.Ergo WinGet package
data.body:
## Goal
- Establish the external repository and credentials required by the automated publisher.
- Submit v1.2.0 as the first `Sandover.Ergo` package to the WinGet community repository.

## Context
- The v1.2.0 Windows AMD64 and ARM64 ZIPs are already published as immutable GitHub Release assets.
- No `sandover/winget-pkgs` fork exists yet.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fork `microsoft/winget-pkgs` to `sandover/winget-pkgs` and keep its default branch aligned with upstream `master`.
- Create the narrowest practical token that can push manifest branches to the fork and open upstream pull requests; store it as the `WINGET_TOKEN` GitHub Actions secret in `sandover/ergo`.
- Generate the initial v1.2.0 manifests for both Windows architectures using the published ZIP URLs and SHA-256 hashes.
- `winget validate` passes, and the manifest is tested with the upstream Windows Sandbox test path.
- Open the initial pull request against `microsoft/winget-pkgs:master` and record its URL in the task completion note.
- Do not publish a new Ergo version solely to test this setup.

## Validation Gates
- `winget validate <manifest-directory>`
- `powershell .\Tools\SandboxTest.ps1 <manifest-directory>` from a current `winget-pkgs` checkout on Windows.
- Confirm the upstream pull request's automated validation checks pass or, if upstream review requires changes, mark this task blocked with the pull request URL and exact requested change.

data.title: Verify and document the WinGet install path
data.body:
## Goal
- Prove that users can discover, install, run, upgrade, and uninstall Ergo through the public WinGet catalog.
- Advertise the command only after the upstream package is available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `winget show --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` resolves the approved package from the public community source.
- On a clean Windows AMD64 environment, `winget install --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` puts `ergo.exe` on `PATH`, and `ergo version` reports v1.2.0.
- Installation is also verified on Windows ARM64, or the task records concrete evidence that WinGet selects the ARM64 manifest correctly when physical ARM64 validation is unavailable.
- `winget uninstall --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` succeeds without leaving the executable on `PATH`.
- README installation instructions add the exact WinGet command while retaining the direct ZIP and `go install` paths.
- A future patch release dry run confirms GoReleaser would update the same package ID rather than create a second package.

## Validation Gates
- Run the exact `winget show`, `winget install`, `ergo version`, and `winget uninstall` commands above on Windows.
- `task ci`

## Scope
- Add blocking native Windows coverage to the existing CI test matrix.
- Automate WinGet community-manifest pull requests through the existing GoReleaser tag workflow.
- Bootstrap and verify the first public WinGet package after the repository changes are approved.

## Decisions
- Package identifier: `Sandover.Ergo`.
- Publisher: `sandover`.
- Use the existing Windows AMD64 and ARM64 ZIP release artifacts.
- Use GoReleaser's native WinGet publisher and a `sandover/winget-pkgs` fork; do not add a second manifest-generation script.
- Keep package versions, URLs, and checksums derived from GoReleaser artifacts.

## Constraints
- Windows CI must be blocking before the initial WinGet submission.
- Credentials live only in GitHub Actions secrets and use the narrowest practical permissions.
- Do not disturb the current GitHub Release or Homebrew outputs.
- The initial WinGet submission targets the already-published v1.2.0 assets; do not cut a release solely to test automation.

## Non-goals
- MSI, MSIX, Microsoft Store, Scoop, or Chocolatey packaging.
- Unrelated release-process or Homebrew changes.

## Goal
- Prove that users can discover, install, run, and uninstall Ergo through the public WinGet catalog.
- Advertise the command only after the upstream package is available.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `winget show --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` resolves the approved package from the public community source.
- On a clean Windows AMD64 environment, `winget install --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` puts `ergo.exe` on `PATH`, and `ergo version` reports v1.2.0.
- Installation is also verified on Windows ARM64, or the task records concrete evidence that WinGet selects the ARM64 manifest correctly when physical ARM64 validation is unavailable.
- `winget uninstall --id Sandover.Ergo --exact` succeeds without leaving the executable on `PATH`.
- README installation instructions add the exact WinGet command while retaining the direct ZIP and `go install` paths.
- A future patch-release dry run confirms GoReleaser would update the same package ID rather than create a second package.

## Validation Gates
- Run the exact `winget show`, `winget install`, `ergo version`, and `winget uninstall` commands above on Windows.
- `task ci`

data.title: v2.0.0: Model-idiomatic command surface
Land the v2 command and compatibility contract
data.body:
## Goal
- Adopt `docs/model-idiomatic-command-vocabulary.md` as the accepted v2 target before implementation begins.
- Make every command postcondition, JSON obligation, and legacy behavior precise enough to test.

## Context
- The proposal is the source design for v2.0.0 and is currently untracked.
- `docs/spec.md` remains the shipped v1 contract until the implementation cutover.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The proposal is marked accepted but unimplemented and contains no unresolved concerns or contradictory transition rules.
- It specifies `claim`, `done`, `block`, `cancel`, `release`, `title`, `body`, and `move`; removes `set` and `reopen`; and prohibits new `error` states.
- It specifies lazy, lossless handling of legacy `error`, claimed-blocked tasks, compaction, and older writers.
- It identifies the deliberate loss of closed-to-unclaimed-`todo` as a public operation.

## Validation Gates
- `git diff --check`
- `rg -n "Open concerns|completion_command|generic state setter" docs/model-idiomatic-command-vocabulary.md`

data.title: Refactor lifecycle mutation core around command postconditions
data.body:
## Goal
- Replace the fixed transition-table implementation with one shared mutation path driven by command postconditions.
- Enforce the v2 claim invariant without duplicating state, claim, body, or result logic across verbs.

## Context
- Follow the accepted contract in `docs/model-idiomatic-command-vocabulary.md`.
- Do not change the public command surface in this task.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The shared core can atomically apply a target state, claim update, optional body replacement, and optional result attachment under the existing lock.
- `doing` requires exactly one claim; every other forward state is unclaimed.
- Same-state calls suppress redundant state events while still emitting body, result, or legacy claim-cleanup events.
- Direct state commands can establish their postcondition from any leaf-task state, including legacy `error`.
- Container and result-path invariants remain centralized and unchanged unless the v2 contract explicitly changes them.
- Unit tests cover postconditions, true no-ops, mixed event batches, and rollback on validation failure.

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./internal/ergo -run 'Test.*(State|Claim|Set|Mutation|Result)'`
- `go test -race ./internal/ergo`

data.title: Implement lazy legacy normalization and lossless compaction
data.body:
## Goal
- Keep old Ergo plans usable without an eager migration or semantic guessing.
- Normalize legacy lifecycle combinations only when a direct v2 lifecycle command states new intent.

## Context
- Legacy logs may contain `error`, claimed `blocked` tasks, `events.jsonl`, and historical event shapes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing legacy fixtures replay through `show`, `list`, readiness, dependency, and container paths unchanged.
- Lifecycle mutation supports every normalization in the accepted contract, including `release error -> todo` with claim clearing.
- A repeated `block` on a legacy claimed-blocked task clears the claim rather than reporting a false no-op.
- `compact` preserves unresolved `error` and claimed-blocked conditions exactly and writes normalized state only after explicit lifecycle mutation.
- Logs appended by an older binary after v2 remains readable on the next v2 invocation.
- No migration command, event version, or repository-open rewrite is added.

## Validation Gates
- `go test -race ./internal/ergo -run 'Test.*(Legacy|Replay|Compact|Migration)'`
- `go test -race ./...`

data.title: Implement lifecycle verbs and resumable claim
data.body:
## Goal
- Add the model-idiomatic agent loop: `claim`, then `done`, `block`, `cancel`, or `release`.
- Make a specific claim resume closed or legacy work instead of rejecting it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `claim` without an ID selects only the oldest ready `todo` task and remains race-safe.
- A specific claim accepts `todo`, `blocked`, `done`, `canceled`, and legacy `error`; same-agent retry is idempotent and a different agent conflicts.
- `done`, `block`, and `cancel` accept any leaf-task state, establish their named state, and clear the claim.
- `release` accepts `doing`, `blocked`, and legacy `error`, returns the task to unclaimed `todo`, and treats unclaimed `todo` as idempotent.
- All four lifecycle verbs support piped body replacement plus `--result` and `--summary` in one atomic batch.
- Claim JSON returns exact task-specific `next_commands` for all four valid exits.
- Human and JSON outputs contain no instructional prose that names a nonexistent command.

## Validation Gates
- `go test -race ./internal/ergo ./cmd/ergo`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo --help`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo quickstart`

data.title: Implement content verbs and remove set
data.body:
## Goal
- Complete the public cutover with `title`, `body`, and `move`, then remove the generic `set` command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `title <id> <title>` replaces only the title and rejects blank titles.
- `body <id>` uses piped stdin literally, supports an empty body, and rejects TTY input with a working example.
- `move <id> <container-id>` and `move <id> --root` enforce destination existence, promotion eligibility, one-level nesting, ancestry, dependency, and container rules.
- The public `set` command and positional mutation JSON are removed without leaving an alias.
- `new task` cannot create `error`; valid creation and claim behavior remain intact.
- Removed `set` and guessed `reopen` syntax fail non-zero with exact direct-command hints.
- Parent commands reject unexpected arguments instead of printing help and succeeding.
- Embedded help and quickstart remain accurate throughout the cutover.

## Validation Gates
- `go test -race ./internal/ergo ./cmd/ergo`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo set ABCDEF '{}'` returns non-zero with the v2 hint
- `go run ./cmd/ergo reopen ABCDEF` returns non-zero with the claim hint
- `go run ./cmd/ergo --help`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo quickstart`

data.title: Add black-box v2 command conformance coverage
data.body:
## Goal
- Prove the complete v2 surface through the real CLI rather than only model helpers.
- Turn observed model shortcuts and state-machine failures into permanent regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A table-driven integration suite covers every lifecycle verb from every forward state and relevant legacy states.
- Coverage includes `todo -> done`, `todo -> canceled`, `done -> doing` through claim, `done -> canceled`, blocked resumption, release paths, idempotent retries, and claim conflicts.
- Tests cover body stdin, empty body, result provenance, late results, JSON `updated_fields`, and true no-op output.
- Tests prove `set`, `reopen`, direct `error` creation, invalid container mutation, and malformed arguments fail with exact actionable hints.
- Tests reproduce the prior duplicate-task trigger: claiming a done task succeeds and reuses the original ID.
- Test fixtures exercise both `plans.jsonl` and legacy `events.jsonl` without mutating real user repositories.

## Validation Gates
- `go test -race ./cmd/ergo ./internal/ergo`
- `task ci`

data.title: Review and improve the shipped Ergo planning skill for v2
data.body:
## Goal
- Make `skills/ergo-feature-planning/SKILL.md` a concise, reliable guide to planning and executing work with the final v2 interface.
- Improve the workflow guidance, not merely replace command names.

## Context
- Preserve and reconcile the current uncommitted edits that remove the blanket sequential-mutation rule and special `.ergo` commit ritual; do not overwrite them.
- Validate the skill against the final v2 help and quickstart rather than the installed binary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The skill uses `claim`, `done`, `block`, `cancel`, and `release`; it never instructs agents to use `set`, `reopen`, or forward `error`.
- Completion guidance distinguishes result files from body notes and shows the exact v2 result flags.
- Blocked work and retryable failed attempts lead to different, accurate commands.
- The planning workflow still resolves design decisions early, maximizes safe task parallelism, and produces smaller-model-friendly tasks.
- Bootstrap and execution guidance fit the repo's actual ownership and commit conventions without restoring the removed blanket rules.
- A cold-read review confirms that a model can plan, claim, finish, block, and release work using only the skill plus the two canonical manuals.

## Validation Gates

data.title: Prepare the v2.0.0 release
data.body:
## Goal
- Produce a release-ready v2.0.0 commit and evidence package without publishing a tag.
- Make the breaking upgrade and legacy compatibility behavior explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `CHANGELOG.md` contains a v2.0.0 entry covering removed `set`, direct verbs, claim resumption, removal of forward `error`, result syntax, JSON changes, and legacy-plan behavior.
- Upgrade notes map every routine v1 `set` intent to its v2 command and state the deliberate absence of unassigned reopening.
- `task ci`, a source build, and a GoReleaser configuration check and snapshot release all pass.
- Snapshot artifacts cover every currently configured OS and architecture and preserve Homebrew plus any WinGet publisher merged before release.
- Fresh-install smoke tests verify version, help, quickstart, JSON cleanliness, and a complete claim-to-exit loop.
- Compatibility smoke tests run against copied real-world legacy logs containing `error`, claimed-blocked tasks, and `events.jsonl`.
- The release commit, CI result, artifact inventory, and remaining external prerequisites are summarized for approval; no tag is pushed in this task.

## Validation Gates
- `task ci`
- `task build`
- `goreleaser check`
- `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean`
- `./bin/ergo --version`

data.title: Publish and verify v2.0.0
data.body:
## Goal
- Publish the approved v2.0.0 tag and prove the released artifacts and install paths deliver the accepted contract.

## Checkpoint
- Produce: the exact release commit, green CI links, changelog excerpt, GoReleaser snapshot inventory, and legacy compatibility results from the preparation task.
- Then ask: permission to create and push tag `v2.0.0`.
- Do not publish the tag without explicit approval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The approved commit is on a clean, current `main` branch before tagging.
- Tag `v2.0.0` triggers the release workflow successfully.
- GitHub Release contains checksums and every configured macOS, Linux, and Windows archive.
- Homebrew installs v2.0.0; WinGet is verified too if its publisher is configured when the tag is cut.
- A freshly installed binary reports v2.0.0 and its help, quickstart, JSON output, direct verbs, and legacy-log behavior match the accepted contract.
- Release failures are corrected with a new version rather than moving or replacing the published tag.

## Validation Gates
- `gh run list --workflow release.yml --limit 3`
- `gh release view v2.0.0`
- `brew update && brew reinstall sandover/tap/ergo`
- `ergo --version`
- `ergo --help`
- `ergo quickstart`

## Scope
- Ship the accepted model-idiomatic command contract as v2.0.0.
- Replace public set mutations with claim, done, block, cancel, release, title, body, and move.
- Preserve legacy plans losslessly and normalize legacy lifecycle state only after explicit intent.
- Rewrite the complete manuals and improve the shipped planning skill.
- Rehearse, publish, and verify the major release across configured distribution channels.

## Decisions
- Version: v2.0.0, following v1.2.0.
- Compatibility: hard command cutover; no set or reopen alias.
- State: todo, doing, blocked, done, canceled; error remains readable but is not created.
- Claims: doing if and only if claimed; a specific claim resumes blocked, closed, or legacy-error work.
- Mutation semantics: direct lifecycle verbs establish postconditions instead of exposing a transition grammar.
- Migration: no eager rewrite or migration command; replay and compact remain lossless.
- Release: no external prerelease tag; require full local snapshot rehearsal, green CI, and explicit approval before v2.0.0 is tagged.
- Packaging: the separate WinGet epic does not block v2; verify every publisher configured when v2 ships.

## Non-goals
- Redesign new task, plan, dependency commands, event types, or storage naming beyond what the v2 contract requires.
- Add arbitrary container nesting or a second mutation interface.
- Preserve closed-to-unclaimed-todo as a public operation.

## Risks
- Legacy error and claimed-blocked logs must remain readable without silent semantic changes.
- Broad direct-state postconditions must not weaken claim races, atomicity, or container invariants.
- Help, quickstart, stable spec, README, and the shipped skill can drift during a large command cutover.
- A breaking release can strand agents if error hints or upgrade guidance are incomplete.

data.title: Implement title and body verbs

data.body:
## Goal
- Add direct title and body editing without exposing generic field mutation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `title <id> <title>` replaces only the title and rejects blank or whitespace-only titles.
- `body <id>` uses piped stdin literally, supports an empty body, and rejects TTY input with a working pipe example.
- Containers and leaf tasks support both commands.
- Both commands use the shared mutation core and emit one stable JSON object with accurate `updated_fields`.
- Same-value title and body updates follow the shared no-op policy without redundant events.

## Validation Gates

data.title: Implement move and placement validation
data.body:
## Goal
- Replace direct parent-field mutation with an explicit move command.
- Make placement validation complete before the generic set path is removed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `move <id> <container-id>` and `move <id> --root` are mutually exclusive and atomic.
- The destination must exist and may be an existing container.
- A root todo task with no claim or results may become a container by receiving its first child.
- Moving a container, moving into self, nested containment, ancestry conflicts, and dependency invariant violations are rejected clearly.
- Moving to the current parent is a true no-op with stable human and JSON output.
- The command uses the shared mutation and locking path.

## Validation Gates
- `go test -race ./internal/ergo ./cmd/ergo -run 'Test.*(Move|Epic|Container|Ancestry)'`
- Exercise move-to-container, promotion, move-to-root, no-op, and each rejection against `go run ./cmd/ergo` in a disposable fixture.

- Help and quickstart provide complete, succinct coverage of every v2 command and flag without equivalent mutation paths.
- `docs/spec.md` becomes the stable v2 contract and incorporates the accepted proposal without duplicating conflicting truth.
- `docs/model-idiomatic-command-vocabulary.md` is retired once `docs/spec.md` absorbs it: either delete it, or demote it to a dated, non-normative design note headed "Superseded by docs/spec.md". Exactly one live v2 contract remains.
- Readiness, explicit blocking, claim ownership, legacy `error`, lazy normalization, and compaction are described consistently.
- README examples use direct verbs and explain the v1-to-v2 cutover plainly.
- Historical changelog entries remain unchanged.
- Every documented shell example is checked against a source build.

## Validation Gates
- `rg -n "ergo (set|reopen)|blocked may.*claim|MUST mark it done" README.md docs/spec.md docs/architecture.md docs/release.md skills/ergo-feature-planning/SKILL.md internal/ergo/help.txt internal/ergo/quickstart.txt` returns no stale forward guidance.
- `! rg -n "MUST mark it done" cmd/ergo internal/ergo` confirms no stale claim-reminder prose survives in Go source.
- Review every remaining `error` reference in the same files and confirm it is explicitly legacy or a generic command failure.
- `go run ./cmd/ergo --help`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo quickstart`
- `task ci`

## Completion
- Rewrote help and quickstart as complete v2 manuals, made docs/spec.md the sole live contract, and retired the proposal.
- Updated architecture, release guidance, README, and shipped hooks; exercised every documented command family against a source build and passed task ci.

## Goal
- Make `skills/ergo-feature-planning/SKILL.md` a concise, reliable guide to planning and executing work with the final v2 interface.
- Improve the workflow guidance, not merely replace command names.

## Context
- Preserve and reconcile the current uncommitted edits that remove the blanket sequential-mutation rule and special `.ergo` commit ritual; do not overwrite them.
- Validate the skill against the final v2 help and quickstart rather than the installed binary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The skill uses `claim`, `done`, `block`, `cancel`, and `release`; it never instructs agents to use `set`, `reopen`, or forward `error`.
- Completion guidance distinguishes result files from body notes and shows the exact v2 result flags.
- Blocked work and retryable failed attempts lead to different, accurate commands.
- The planning workflow still resolves design decisions early, maximizes safe task parallelism, and produces smaller-model-friendly tasks.
- Bootstrap and execution guidance fit the repo ownership and commit conventions without restoring the removed blanket rules.
- A cold-read review confirms that a model can plan, claim, finish, block, and release work using only the skill plus the two canonical manuals.

## Validation Gates
- `! rg -n 'ergo (set|reopen)|state.?=.?(error)|mark.*error|Do not include \\.ergo|sequentially and synchronously' skills/ergo-feature-planning/SKILL.md`
- Run every Ergo command shown in the skill against `go run ./cmd/ergo` in a disposable fixture.
- Perform one cold-read planning and execution walkthrough using only the skill, `ergo --help`, and `ergo quickstart`; record the commands exercised in the task completion note.
- `task ci`

## Completion
- Rewrote the skill around the v2 intent commands while preserving the removal of blanket mutation serialization and the special .ergo commit ritual.
- Cold-read walkthrough exercised: help, quickstart, init, where, new task, plan, sequence, title, move, claim oldest, claim by ID, show, body, done --result --summary, block with a body note, release --result --summary, and cancel.
- Distinguished body notes from real result files and block from retryable release; the skill validator and task ci pass.

## Goal
- Produce a release-ready v2.0.0 commit and evidence package without publishing a tag.
- Make the breaking upgrade and legacy compatibility behavior explicit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `CHANGELOG.md` contains a v2.0.0 entry covering removed `set`, direct verbs, claim resumption, removal of forward `error`, result syntax, JSON changes, and legacy-plan behavior.
- Upgrade notes map every routine v1 `set` intent to its v2 command and state the deliberate absence of unassigned reopening.
- `task ci`, the normal source build, a candidate-version build, GoReleaser configuration check, and snapshot release all pass.
- The candidate binary is built with the same version linker variable used by GoReleaser, injected as `2.0.0`, and `version` reports `ergo 2.0.0`; the release tag remains `v2.0.0`.
- Snapshot artifacts cover every currently configured OS and architecture and preserve Homebrew plus any WinGet publisher merged before release.
- Candidate-binary smoke tests verify help, quickstart, JSON cleanliness, and a complete claim-to-exit loop.
- Compatibility smoke tests run against copied real-world legacy logs containing `error`, claimed-blocked tasks, and `events.jsonl`.
- The release commit, remote CI result, artifact inventory, and remaining external prerequisites are summarized for approval; no tag is created or pushed in this task.

## Validation Gates
- `task ci`
- `task build`
- `mkdir -p .scratch/release`
- `go build -ldflags "-s -w -X main.version=2.0.0" -o .scratch/release/ergo-v2-candidate ./cmd/ergo`
- `.scratch/release/ergo-v2-candidate version | rg '^ergo 2\\.0\\.0$'`
- `goreleaser check`
- `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean`
- Push the release commit through the normal review path and record passing remote CI for that exact commit.

## Completion
- Added the v2.0.0 changelog and complete v1-to-v2 upgrade mapping, including the deliberate absence of unassigned reopen.
- Migrated the deprecated GoReleaser formula publisher to a Homebrew cask; no WinGet publisher is configured.
- Passed `task ci`, `task build`, `goreleaser check`, and `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean`.
- Built the release candidate through GoReleaser's `main.version` linker variable and confirmed `ergo 2.0.0`.
- Produced six snapshot archives: darwin, linux, and windows on amd64 and arm64, plus checksums and the Homebrew cask.
- Exercised help, quickstart, clean JSON, results, and done, block, release, resume, and cancel with the candidate binary.
- Copied the live plan to legacy `events.jsonl`, added v1 `error` and claimed-blocked records, proved compaction preserved both, and proved v2 normalized them correctly.
- Pushed release content commit `199831010b5d6172db3c99844b245de734c4060f`; CI run 29793457354 passed all four jobs.
- No release tag was created. Explicit approval and the configured Homebrew tap token remain publication prerequisites.

data.title: Remove obsolete set terminology from v2 tests
data.body:
## Goal
- Remove obsolete `TestSet_*` names and v1-shaped fixture terminology from the v2 test suite without losing behavioral coverage.

## Context
- Several historical integration tests now route through direct v2 commands, but their names still imply that `set` exists.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are named for the v2 behavior or command they exercise.
- Shared fixtures expose explicit v2 operations; legacy event injection remains clearly labeled and limited to compatibility setup.
- No test can make removed `set` appear supported.

## Validation Gates
- `! rg -n 'func TestSet_|runSetTask|runLegacyMutationFixture' cmd/ergo --glob '*_test.go'`
- `task ci`

## Goal
- Publish the approved v2.0.0 tag and prove the workflow, release assets, and install paths deliver the accepted contract.

## Checkpoint
- Produce: the exact release commit, green CI links, changelog excerpt, GoReleaser snapshot inventory, candidate-version output, and legacy compatibility results from the preparation task.
- Then ask: permission to create and push tag `v2.0.0`.
- Do not publish the tag without explicit approval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The approved commit is on a clean, current `main` branch before tagging.
- Tag `v2.0.0` triggers the release workflow, and the tag-specific workflow run completes successfully.
- GitHub Release is final rather than draft or prerelease and contains `checksums.txt` plus all six configured macOS, Linux, and Windows archives.
- Every downloaded release archive matches `checksums.txt`.
- Homebrew installs version 2.0.0. Verification invokes `$(brew --prefix)/bin/ergo` explicitly so an existing Go-bin installation cannot mask the formula binary.
- WinGet is verified too if its publisher is configured when the tag is cut.
- A freshly installed release binary reports `ergo 2.0.0` through the `version` subcommand; help, quickstart, JSON output, direct verbs, and legacy-log behavior match the accepted contract.
- Release failures are corrected with a new version rather than moving or replacing the published tag.

## Validation Gates
- `gh run list --workflow release.yml --limit 10 --json databaseId,headBranch,conclusion,url` and identify the run whose `headBranch` is `v2.0.0`.
- `gh run watch <tag-specific-run-id> --exit-status`
- `gh release view v2.0.0 --json tagName,isDraft,isPrerelease,assets,url` and assert the release is final and lists `checksums.txt` plus the six expected OS/architecture archives.
- `gh release download v2.0.0 --pattern 'checksums.txt' --pattern 'ergo_2.0.0_*' --dir .scratch/release-v2`
- From `.scratch/release-v2`, run `shasum -a 256 -c checksums.txt`.
- `brew update`
- If `brew list --versions ergo` succeeds, run `brew reinstall sandover/tap/ergo`; otherwise run `brew install sandover/tap/ergo`.
- `$(brew --prefix)/bin/ergo version | rg '^ergo 2\\.0\\.0$'`
- Run help, quickstart, one complete lifecycle smoke, and one copied-legacy-log smoke with `$(brew --prefix)/bin/ergo`.

## Completion
- Created and pushed annotated tag `v2.0.0` at `18b629f3525756adb109a4f9a23ad1553890e6d9` after exact-commit CI run 29798811573 passed.
- Release workflow 29798855233 passed and published a final, non-prerelease GitHub Release.
- Verified `checksums.txt` and all six darwin, linux, and windows archives; every downloaded checksum passed.
- Replaced the generated commit dump with a crisp 205-word release note explaining the new vocabulary, common upgrades, and legacy compatibility.
- Published the Homebrew cask, then repaired formula-to-cask resolution in tap commit `da95e3d37d6580ac63cec3a723b76a982cb06bcf` without moving the release tag.
- `brew install sandover/tap/ergo` installed cask 2.0.0, `brew audit --cask --online` passed, and `/opt/homebrew/bin/ergo version` reported `ergo 2.0.0`.
- The installed binary passed help, quickstart, clean JSON, result attachment, claim, done, block, release, resume, cancel, and show checks.
- A copied `events.jsonl` graph created by v1 preserved legacy `error` and claimed-blocked records across v2 compaction, then normalized them correctly through `release` and `block`.
- No WinGet publisher was configured, so no WinGet installation path applied to this release.

data.title: Refine v2 manuals around lifecycle intent and exact examples
data.body:
## Goal
- Make `ergo --help` and `ergo quickstart` teach the v2 lifecycle by intent, with exact examples and less repeated state-machine detail.

## Context
- The primary users are models, and these two outputs are the complete manual.
- The current help agent loop claims a task and then shows `done --result`, but its sample `updated_fields` omits `claim`; the real response is `claim`, `result`, and `state`.
- The command vocabulary is now stable, but choosing among `done`, `block`, `cancel`, and `release` still requires reading several separated paragraphs.
- Documentation calls parents containers while the public JSON field and filter remain `epic`; this compatibility naming should be explicit so models do not invent `container` input.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correct the claimed-task completion JSON example so it matches the actual event fields and ordering.
- Put one compact intent-first exit guide near the agent loop: objective met means `done`; identified impediment means `block`; objective no longer wanted means `cancel`; valid unfinished work ready for another attempt means `release`.
- State plainly that a specific claim deliberately bypasses oldest-ready selection and may resume work with unmet dependencies; automatic claim remains readiness-gated.
- Explain once that documentation says container while the placement field and list filter retain the name `epic`.
- Consolidate repeated legacy and invariant explanations so the main workflow stays prominent without losing any supported behavior.
- Keep help and quickstart at or below their current lengths while retaining complete command, flag, input, JSON, placement, compatibility, and maintenance coverage.
- Validate every command and JSON example against the source-built CLI rather than an installed binary.

## Validation Gates
- `go run ./cmd/ergo --help`
- `go run ./cmd/ergo quickstart`
- Exercise every changed command and JSON example in a disposable graph.
- `task ci`
